title: DO NOT BUY THESE - unless you like disappointment.
text:
I received my first pair of these headphones last November. I'd never spent this kind of money on myself for such a luxury item. When they arrived I was over the moon because the sound quality was amazing. About six weeks later, I took them off my head and place them on a side table. And I am very careful with the things I own. I never had much money growing up, so you learn to take care of your possessions. Anyways when I went to use them again, the left ear had broken off of the head band and was hanging from the wires. I contacted Sony who's response was, we don't cover damage. WHAT? I argued with them for quite a while and a few days later they agreed to replace them.

Fast forward a few months later. Now I am EXTRA carful with my second pair because of the problems with the first pair. I am literally removing the headphones from my ears when the same exact thing happens again. There was NO WAY that I broke them. I have all the parts (see image) and the plast

title: Very Solid For Out-of-Office
text:
I left a lengthy review of the Jabra Evolve2 85 (E285) vs Bose Quiet Comfort 35 II (QC35II) vs Bose Noise Cancelling 700 (B700) vs Sony WH-1000XM4 (SX4) (in order of when I received them) for the E285. Since that review is out there, I'll reduce this one only to E285 and SX4.

Microphone and Playback
The E285 was notably better than the SX4, as evidenced by my recordings while drumming. It’s neck and neck as far as clarity of voice goes.

Akin to the E285, the SX4 are quite good as far as pick-up goes. But unfortunately, they were a little too good (or simply worse at distinguishing background noise). A co-worker and I had a Zoom meeting with ourselves in the same closet (to maximize productivity), switching between the headsets. Whoever had the E285 could hear themselves through the headset. At first, I thought it was because of the function where you hear yourself through the headset. But alas, upon one of us leaving the room, the echo went away. This meant t

title: Great Sound - 1st Class Noise Cancelling
text:
First a side note. Do NOT order this item for overnight delivery. It is a password protected delivery which means that someone will be at your door at 4:00 AM asking for the magic incantation.

I have the comparable Sony earbuds so I knew what to expect. Others will have to contend with micro-printed docs on 5 lb paper and a weird wordless diagram which requires a translation from pantomime to one's native language. HOWEVER, the Sony website has ample documentation in a readable format. Also, you need the Sony headphone app to fine tune the setup.

I have acute PTSD, a consequence of gun violence. My building is undergoing major construction in th4e form of concrete restoration. I suspect that, for most people, the noise from the machines is a mere inconvenience. For me that is not the case. It's a pile driver inside my head.

My earbuds were doing the job (these products have the best noise-cancelling of any device I have tried). However, they have limite

title: A worthwhile successor to the incredible M3 with some upgrades we were waiting for!!
text:
It’s finally here and a worthwhile successor to the amazing M3. So I will start by saying that currently my go to headphones are the M3 but my favorite headphones over the last 5 years have been the Bose QC 35 II. These however are going to be my main pair especially for use doing work from home.
The battery seems to charge in about the same time and have the same life span which is around 30 hours and pretty incredible. So here is what I have noticed listening to the M3 versus the M4. I am glad they kept the M3 design which I personally like more than the Bose 700. The ear cushion feels a little more comfortable and is a little bigger making the comfortable M3 just a little more comfortable but there doesn’t seem to be a huge difference. Hopefully the padding holds up as well as the M3 has as mine still are incredible shape despite a lot of use. The M4 also changed the NC/Ambient button on the M3 to a Custom which for me was not really necessary but it will allow you to mak

title: Great headphones with one major flaw (In my opinion)
text:
I bought a paid of the WH-1000XM4's and Bose NC 700's trying to decide which one was better. I prefer the Sony headphones in terms of customization and sound. However, I will be returning these due to the headband and ear cups. The headband puts a lot of pressure on the crown of my head, no matter how I adjust it, causing pain and headaches if wearing for more than an hour or so. Regarding the ear cups, they are very shallow and I can feel my ears touching the inner portion of the headphones. This puts pressure on my ears, so that after a couple hours it becomes uncomfortable as well.

For some reason, the Bose NC 700's do not have these pressure issues for me. I cannot say it will be the same for everyone, but I will be going with the 700's specifically for this reason.

title: Sound good but Very cheep plastic design & cups are totally cheep quality
text: [[VIDEOID:e8e7e82da599c03813f83505d668e7d8]] Only things I like about Sony set is sound ,  crap design & no durability

title: Tons of options to control these AND can lay down with them!!!
text:
So, a lot of people complained about the headphones pausing for voice etc.. there's an app you can download where you can turn off auto pausing, auto turn off, and you can even turn off the noise cancelling. I personally can't handle the noise cancelling aspect, as it hurts my ears and gives me weird head pressure.. so I'm super happy I can turn that off.

My headphones lasted about 3 days, listening about 4-6 hours per day. The headphones only take about 30 minutes to charge as well.
They are suuuuuper comfortable.

I am a smaller person, and a big issue I have with most headphones is they're way too big for my head, so I can't lean my head back onto the bed or couch with them on.
Not with these! I can lean my head back FINALLY, and I barely feel like I'm wearing them.

I had beats before this, and they hurt my ears and my head because they were so weirdly shaped and tight. Like my ears would literally fall asleep. I seriously don't even notice

title: Not so fast
text:
Tragically there are very few things I'm an expert in, but sound is one. I listen to 5 hours of music every day on headphones on full volume and I've done that for 25+ years. I've had at least 120 headphones, literally.

After watching countless youtube reviews praising the XM4 as the best NC headphones money could buy today I finally picked one up. I'm now highly suspicious of those reviews. They were likely paid for or perhaps free headphones were given out. The one thing the youtubers know you can't do is listen for yourself. If you actually heard the sound coming out of these headphones it would be instant rejection. The hype is just that. Kudos to Sony's marketing team.

That's not to say this is a bad product. The engineering is top notch. The app is perfect. Hardware is well designed and built to last. Battery seems to never die. Exceptionally light. Stylish. They got everything right except for the sound. (!)

I think these are low quality speakers

title: Expensive - But worth it
text:
So first off - THE STUFF THAT IS GREAT

Components:
I've worked in the audio industry for years. I know the costs that go into building headsets, etc. Sony has not skimped on this front.

Fit:
I've got a big noggin and wear glasses. I'm able to use these for multiple hours on work calls and it's VERy easy to forget I have them on.

Active noise cancellation (ANC):
I've owned multiple sets of ANC headphones in the past (Bose, Creative, etc). Sony has hands down the best ANC profile I've ever heard. Honestly, I was surprised as how good it is. I expected "more of the same" but was pleasantly surprised as how adaptive the ANC on these headphones are. Genuinely impressed.

Audio Quality:
Great punch on the low end. It's not earth-shattering but has a great feel to them - especially impressive since they are wireless. Mid-range and highs are quite good with a little bit of muddiness in the upper ranges but this is going to vary on

title: Not an upgrade but still ok
text:
I own the previous XM3s. The new XM4s have less bass and are more clear but I actually preffer how the XM3s sound due to their bass. The music sounds more alive on the XM3s.

Also they removed the Arena, Concert Hall, etc modes from the Sony app which was my favorite thing when watching movies (I used to put arena mode and it sounded like a movie theatre). Because of this, the headphones aren’t very good for consuming media as they just sound flat.

The new improvements are bluetooth 5.0, ability to pause music when you speak, a sensor that pauses music when you take off the headphones, and multi point pairing and uhmm thats about it.

These are a huge disappointment tbh, this is more of an update not an upgrade. You could have just released this and still called it XM3 Pro or something. The headphones look exactly the same, they have no meaningful features, and the button layout is outdated (why do i have to press and hold for 5 seconds to turn on and off

title: It's not silver
text:
Great headset, Just as awesome as the WH1000XM3s, but the color is definitely not silver.

My biggest gripes:

- The color isn't silver (oh, and there isn't a white option)
- The mic appears to be on at all times. This is actually a firmware issue, the later firmware versions on the XM3s act exactly the same, this makes it appear that the headset has weaker noise canceling when it does not)
- The XM3s actually have a somewhat defective headband. There are pressure points on the headband that will lead to it breaking after a certain period of time. After some testing I can confirm this isn't resolved by the XM4s. Note that this type of damage isn't covered under warranty. Many of us have fought with Sony to the point of switching to Bose, Apple, etc. If your headset band snaps, good luck.
- The quality is too low for the price. I actually don't have any issue with the sound quality, but my XM3s didn't last all that long...
- Customer service sucks

title: Battery sucks
text: 30 hours of battery? Fully charged and only 2.5 hrs of use. What happened to the other 27.5 hrs??

title: The sound cancellation is way better than Bose...
text: Got these and the Bose QuietComfort 35 II Wireless Bluetooth Headphones at the same time to compare. The Sony pair is way better, more comfortable, and better looking. The matte grey is a tad beige and looks very classy.  Goes with everything I wear and feels ore neutral than the black. I use these everyday for noise sensitivity issues I experience. I love that I can still hear voices well enough, but it neutralizes background sounds exceptionally. They help a lot with anxiety. As for the sound quality, I am not an audiophile by any means, but to me they sound great when I am listening to music and podcasts. The battery life is also impressive. For me they've been worth every penny. No issues 2 months in with heavy, but gentle usage.

title: Love em 💕💖.
text: Threw my 4 pairs of ear phones 🎧🎧🎧🎧 away. These have superb sound🎵🎶🎻 and the noise cancelling is phenomenal. My wife 👵 hates them since I really can not hear her. Many thanks 👍

title: Broke in less than one year.. arm dislocated
text: Broke in less than a year. I am a 58 year old woman, handling my products with care. The arm broke in less than a year rendering the product useless.  Very disappointed.

title: Almost perfect headphones
text:
Excellent headphones.
Quality of music: 10/10
Noise Cancelling: 9/10
Mic: 8/10
Bluetooth Connectivity: 6/10
Aesthetics: 5/10
I'm an audiophile looking for quality headphones that have the ability to listen to music while being able to jump into zoom meetings w/o changing headsets. I've been a fan of the WF-1000XM3 ear pods and bought these to accompany them. They didn't disappoint. You'll need to spend some time configuring these for features like multi-device bluetooth support. The only issue I have is with the connectivity. Sometimes they don't connect as expected and would have to reconnect. This usually happens with just my macbook and would run into issues where the output works but mic doesn't but reconnecting usually resolves this issue. Aesthetics I marked low b/c they're not visually appealing especially in meetings but that's not my primary reason to buy these. An additional feature I like on these headphones is the ability for them to automati

title: $300 for apair of headphones that died 3months
text: I finally convinced myself to purchase some high end headphones. What a huge mistake. First they never speed charged at all. Such a dissaointment day one first time battery was low i did the 15min charge an nothing if i don’t charge a min 1-2hrs it wont play 10 min. I thought i was doing something wrong than about 2 months of use they started to charge randomly meaning i could charge them 3 hrs pick them up an hear battery low please charge than cut off. Some days it worked same port same charge cable.i started smelling electric burn in my office or so i thuaght untill i noticed the plastic around the headphone charge port was melted. Turns out it was the headphones. Other issues i could answer a call or atleast connect but if i wanted to actually speak to the caller i would have to scramble for my phone to switc call to headphone an atleast 50° of calls would hang up before i got there. So everycall was a phone scramble nightmare. Such a dissapointment. Im waiting to hear from sony su

title: Nearly as good as it gets with headphones
text:
These have a wonderful full ranged and warm sound. Noise cancellation is excellent, but the audio isolation by itself is so good that you really don't need noise cancellation turned on until you are in a truly noisy environment.

These are the most comfortable headphones I have ever bought! I experienced no fatigue from wearing them for more than an hour. If you wear glasses, you don't have to take them off. Many headphones pads are so firm, that it feels like the eyeglass stems are being pressed into your head. Not so with this set of cans. The cups fully surround the ears and don't press heavily on the ears themselves.

The cons.....I was shocked that for a premium set of headphones, that the charging cable was only 8 inches long. This can create logistical issues depending on how close your available USB charging port is and if there is space to safely set them down while charging. There is no excuse to at least offer a 3 foot cable pack in for a premium product

title: Good sounding headphones, but there are some thing you need to know
text: I ordered the blue version of these headphones and expected them to be the blue shown on the website.  They are not, what they ship is called midnight blue, almost indistinguishable from black.  Second and most important, there is no way to change the L/R balance.  If you are hard of hearing in one ear, there is no way to increase the volume on that ear.  I have been forced to use this set wired, whereby the balance can be adjusted by Windows.  Noise cancellation in this case is worthless.  Maybe in the future, Sony will upgrade the phone app to allow for L/R balance adjustments.

title: Superb, But Fussy to Get Dialed-In
text:
I received these a couple of weeks ago and have spent several evenings with them since. I took the entire fist evening (and then some) getting the WH-1000XM4 set up, updated, synced, and dialed in, then settled in for almost 6 hours listening across a variety of music sources and types, ranging from radio to highest-quality, uncompressed audio. I also spent time on the chat function, headset (telephone) functions, and the accompanying app (IOS, in my case). I've used them most nights since then, and they have replaced my usual go-to Sony MDR-V6 set in the bedroom. (I keep another MDR-V6 set in the office, and a nice Panasonic headset in the family room.)

The entire design approach to these headphone is subtlety, and EVERYTHING on the headset is black except the "Sony" on each cup and the rims of the top mic ports, all of which are copper-colored. They are *beautifully* designed and made, every contour blending, every joint free-moving, every surface soft touch. Their c

title: Good for Two Years... and then
text:
I started to get a popping sound in the left channel. I called Sony "Support," and their "solution" was a do a factory reset. Lame, but okay. That didn't work. They offered no other support except for contacting the only repair place in the United States.

If they found a problem, the minimum cost would be $106. I think high-end headphones should last more than two years, and Sony should provide better support after the sale. It looks like am going back to Bose.

title: Great headphones, but so many downsides
text:
Positives:

I do believe these headphones have incredible noise cancelling technology. They manage to make virtually every low-pitched sound (train engines, washing machines, etc.) non-existent and seriously reduce the sound of high-pitched sounds like talking, loud typing, clapping, etc. I'm a bit familiar with what's behind ANC and I can see both the internal and external microphones on the unit. Having ANC tech that adapts to both is what adds to a lot of the cost in high-end ANC headphones. These are certainly state of the art in that respect.

I expected the audio quality to be more amazing. It really is no better than the iPhone's included headphones or even the very cheap (but highly rated) Panasonic earbuds. However, adding ANC to playing sound is a tradeoff. Other, cheaper ANC headphones are far worse and wind up fighting with the music instead of letting you enjoy it. It could be that Sony is simply balancing ANC with sound quality and it can't get better

title: Noise Cancelling on Point, Almost Perfect
text:
Right off the bat these headphones look and feel pretty nice. The quality is pretty good overall, even though its body is made of plastic. The case house the headphones with some internal pockets for the included cables, there’s a net pocket on the outside that can probably only hold something relatively thin or flat. No wall adapter included with these headphones. They feel quite sturdy, haven’t truly abused them just yet, but they seem to handle flexes and movement pretty well so far.

The headphone fit is pretty good, they fit is snug but not overbearing. Headphone doesn’t move when I’m moving. Ears do get a bit warm with long use, but that’s expected.

The battery life on this is amazing, I been listening with it for a couple hours and it barely used up the battery. I also notice that it charges pretty quickly and even with a small amount of charging, I was able to get a couple hours of listening easily.

I’m glad they opted to include the headphone ca

title: Excellent for it's specific purpose, but not really Hi-Fi sound
text:
At this price point, I thought these were going to sound like a pair of Sennheiser wired headphones from 5-10 years ago. Not exactly...

Their sound isolation is very impressive. The iPhone app is also excellent.

But my ~$100 wired gaming headphones from Logitech sound significantly better in a quiet room. My Airpods and my Airpod Pros also generally sound better.

The main issue is a somewhat muddy low to mid bass. I would have originally given it a 4/10, but after playing with some of the settings and the EQ, I think I can give it a 7/10 (considering the price point).

I think if I were on public transit like a bus or train daily, I would give these a 10/10. In a reasonably quiet home, I'm just not 100% what I was hoping for.

title: Best headphones.
text: My new smartphone came with a pre-order bonus of the WF1000XM3's but I don't care for in the ear headphones.  So I gave those to my son and purchased a pair of the WH1000XM4 for myself.  What a great purchase.  The sound quality is terrific and what a way to first experience noise cancellation.  These things are so full of features I still am learning everything they are capable of.  I heartily recommend these headphones.

title: Was expecting much more but these are all hype.
text: I was really expecting a lot more after reading some reviews and watching YouTube videos. However I should know better that gadget Youtubers Really don’t understand Sound quality and are all impressed by how a gadget looks and non sound features. So I should start by saying over all these weren’t bad headphones. But they weren’t really great. Not enough to keep for the price. I had to return these. The biggest annoyance is the noise canceling works ok (Not quite as good as AirPod pro) But it has a really annoying tinny sound As if you just cover your ears with soup cans. Overall the sound quality is average and nothing spectacular. As far as consumer grade they are the same as other overpriced highly market headphones. but they don’t compare to anything high-end like sennheiser, Grado, Beyerdynamic akg or whatever. Bass is clean but not deep. Mid frequencies weak and buried deep. Vocals get lost and guitars have no sparkle or crispness. High frequencies are the same. Kind of not presen

title: Excellent headphones... when they can stay connected
text:
I really, really wanted to like these headphones. I have found that when I am working on my computer that I prefer to use over-the-ear headphones. After my last ~$50 pair broke after 6 months of use, I decided I was going to pay for an upgrade. I was between this pair and a set of Bose, and I decided I would roll with the Sony headphones.

For about a month, these headphones were excellent. Very comfortable on the ears, seamless connection, and the noise canceling worked very well. Then they started randomly disconnecting. I looked into returning them, but after speaking with customer service I was recommended to change some settings in the Sony app to help with the connectivity. This worked for about another month before they started disconnecting again. It'd be one thing if it's one disconnection and then they stay reconnected, but the connection will drop within minutes. It makes using the headphones completely useless.

If Sony can fix this disconnection and ass

title: Horrible UI/connecting issues
text: Wow, I work in tech. These are hyped. The user experience connecting is horrible. Never connected to the device I want. The voice assistant is horrible. The power button doesn’t always work, you never know if they are on or off because no LED tells you. Can’t believe Sony pushed this out. The app sucks and is confusing

title: Really PREMIUM sound!
text:
I've heard a lot of wonderful things about the Sony noise cancelling sets like these, and let me tell you: it isn't just hype. The sound quality is excellent, and the noise cancelling is eerily amazing. I put it on my wife and kids with the noise cancelling off and then turned it on to demonstrate, and you can see each them suddenly smile like "whoa". Like my Sony studio headphones I use professionally for work, the actual fidelity of the sound it reproduces is excellent, and the microphone for calls is excellent despite being up by my ears and so far from my mouth (other bluetooth headphones I've tried were good for music only, the mic being up by my ears wasn't good enough to isolate my voice from ambient sound, but this definitely does a great job).
The app gives a ton of control beyond what I expected to really customize the experience. For example, I have mine set to auto-pause if the headphones are removed, and to turn off noise cancelling when it detects me speaking to som

title: Excellent, but so boomy it gives me a headache
text:
To keep things brief:
- *Everything* is done through a fairly decent mobile app. I'm fine with this, especially since it makes some things very simple (e.g. switching between multiple devices).
- Bluetooth connectivity is stellar. Latency is invisible (FAR better than anything I've tried except the competitive Bose headsets), switching is very quick (like 1s), no stuttering or issues whatsoever.
- It's a little sad / surprising that LDAC isn't supported when using multiple connections... but meh. AAC still sounds great.
- Noice cancelling is absolutely top-notch and incredible. But you already know this.
- EQ is.......... bad. Plain and simple. There's no nicer way to describe it. There are worse EQs out there! But this is well below average.

But.

BUT.

HOLY COW is this "boomy". So much so that it gives me a headache after about an hour at my normal relatively-low listening volume, even without noise-canceling. The Bose

title: The Best Gets Better
text: I’ve owned the 2nd and 3rd gen versions of these headphones and the 4th gen is the best yet. I don’t like that the 3D audio feature is only supported by Tidal and a couple other apps. Other than that nit-picky feedback, I highly recommend.

title: Poor bass
text: Everything but the bass is great. The bass is mediocre. I expect more powerful bass potential from $300 headphones. I’ve EQ tuned to maximize the bass potential and it didn’t get very far.

title: Regret not sticking with Bose
text: These are pretty but just odd. The control functions are weird. I use for work/phone calls and have had multiple complaints that people can’t hear me when I talk so have to yank off my head and switch to phone. Music sounds great however.

title: Absolutely aggravating
text:
I am soooo disappointed with this headphone. I bought my Sony over a Bose because of the reviews. Right out of the box I had major problems.
First, you cannot charge them and keep listening to your program. Plugging the headphones in shuts off Bluetooth. So midway through a movie or such you hear the battery low warning, and plug the headphone in, and dead it goes. In my case, I have to re-pair to myAppleTV or Samsung TV after each charge. Then, somehow, the controls are just where my fingers / thumb grab the headphone to place them on my head. I manage to hit a button that moves my setting to “ambient” mode or over from that, and I fade in and out of my movie. Clear sound for a few minutes, then a click and nothing. I’d much rather use a cheaper headphone my wife bought through a local store that hooks up to the Apple TV and stays there. So many good things with this headphone become such a headache that I lost my temper and threw them away. Several hundred dollars I know, but th

title: There is a reason these headphones are on sale.  Sound was ok, but not what was expected
text:
I was looking for a robust sound and high quality headphone. These are a great idea, but they need more work. I was very disappointed. The sound quality was ok, but when turned all the way up, the volume was lower than the cheap earbuds I use at the gym.
The controls are sketchy and I had a difficult time getting everything to work as advertised.

title: Unusable. Excellent marketing, poor engineering.
text:
I will return these shortly.

I can hear calls but they report me cutting in and out. Carefully tested under controlled circumstances.

Touch controls are straight up disastrous. I never know what is going to happen when I try to control them.

App solution on android... well, just go read the reports there. Users are very unhappy.

The things that work well on the Sony Wh-1000xm4 also work just as well on less expensive headphones.

title: Great set of cans!
text: These are my first pair of noise cancelling headphones and wow do they work well! No more crying babies, barking dogs. Nice silence on airplanes. Only complaint is the sound quality. It’s not bad by any means, just not as balanced as my sennheiser HD598’s but those are open back. After manually adjusting the equalizer they sound better and I’m happy with that.

title: awesome and well worth it
text: I LOVE these headphones.  They are not too heavy, the noise cancellation feature works well, and the sound quality is great. If I could give six stars I would.

title: Great headphones poor google assistant with iPhone
text: I had high hopes for these as they are so highly rated. Please be aware that if you use with an iPhone they won’t start things with google assistant only open the app. That basically defeats the purpose of an assistant and I’m stuck still using AirPod pros with Siri instead. I think if you’re an android user you’ll love these though. The sound quality is very good and so is the noise cancellation. The main reason I wanted this was to be able to switch from Siri to google assistant on my iPhone and unfortunately it’s still too soon for that.

title: Super Long Battery Life!
text:
This is my first pair of high end over the ear noise canceling headphones. I have used 3M over the ear blue tooth ear protection headsets and the sound was good and during construction projects they are still my go-to over the ear protection and music machines but for long flights they worked but were not very comfortable.

So i decided to buy the Best available and got these a few months ago - probably would have bought the Apple ones that just came out but these were and possibly still are the Best. I wanted only the Best because i know i will use them and have them for years.

I am very happy with the purchase - the fit, finish, controls and everything are excellent. The battery life is so long that i Never have to worry about power during a flight or if i get a long long long long layover - they just have a ton of battery power.

I have had no problems with the controls though they take some experience. They tether seemlessly for me to everything i try

title: Horrible Feedback Sound, Sony Made Good
text:
I use these for work. I listen to music and videos during work and use them for some calls and meetings on Zoom.

I've liked the sound and comfort over the last year. I do get some complaints from time to time that people have issues hearing me but hard to tell if the headphones are the culprit.

Starting earlier this week, less than a month outside of the one year warranty, I've been getting a horrible, painfully loud feedback sound in the left earpiece. An online search shows I'm not the only one. Trying some troubleshooting and We'll see how it goes but for the price this isn't great. My wife has a pair of Bose that she's had for well over 5 years with no problems. Literally on her second set of foam ear pads.

***************Update***************

I reached out to Sony and since they were within 30 days of the warranty ending they sent brand new replacements. I had to send the defective set back first, they provided a shipping label, and the

title: Noise Cancelling Really Works, but not great for working out in
text:
I was a bit skeptical if they would work, this was the first noise cancelling headphones I've ever tried. It was pretty amazing. I needed something for the gym because the gym cranks annoying techno and hip hop music. It's terrible. And someone is always vacuuming when I'm there. Activate the noise cancelling and all that drops to background whisper. And then turn on music...and it all disappears. And you don't have to have the music very loud either. Just quiet.

I ended up returning them though and getting the comparable earbuds instead (which are not quite as good at noise cancelling, but seem to be working good enough so far - just need to turn up the music a smidgen louder to block the same level of noise). 2 reasons I returned these over the ear ones:
1) They don't stay on your face real well when doing things like bench pressing or any other movement where you aren't standing and sitting straight up
2) They get really warm having all that over your

title: Best noise canceling
text: These are the best noise canceling headphones I have ever tried.

title: Good headphones just not used ones from amazon
text: I ended up getting a fake used set of these headphones. But that didn't deter me. I went out to Best buy to buy a pair of these headphones brand new. The headphones are awesome. The bass is Poppin. It stops playing when the left ear is moved. I can opt for LDAC or to connect two devices. And these cans sound good regardless if I use them with a cheap mp3 player or my Asus ROG 5. The touch controls are the same as the xm3s and the sound profile is almost the same but the noise canceling is better on this version.

title: Randomly Disconnects
text: Randomly disconnects. The sound quality and noise cancellation was great, but the headphones would randomly disconnect from my iPhone and would not reconnect without a power cycle. Happened on multiple apps without a clear pattern. All software was updated.

title: Sound, fit and features.......
text: Awesome sound and fit and feel like a high-end product. Makes cutting my large yard so much more enjoyable

title: Great headphones
text: Seriously the best noise cancelling cans out there.  They left Bose in the dust.

title: Great for listening to music but.....
text: Comfortable and the ANC works well and the sound is very good. but when I try to use to make or answer a call the other person hears an annoying echo and so do I to a lesser extent.  Also will not work with Zoom or other video Apps.  If you only want to listen to music they are a great deal.

title: Better sound/ANC than Bose, Apple, etc..Not as practical for calls/mobility as AirPods Pro
text: Wow !!! At first I was extremely disappointed that they didn’t seem to sound like the ones I heard on display at a local store, so I researched how to get the best sound out of these so I could hopefully match what I heard.  I succeeded and it was very quick and easy.  The source of your music and quality stream is very very importantThe EQ can work wonders and there are easy tutorials to follow made by bona fide audiophiles that can have your music sounding beautiful, punchy ( just the right amount rather than muffled or muddy).  Just turning off ANC and selecting DSEE extreme (upscales music quality detail through Sony proprietary tech) make a very big difference.  The ANC is wonderful. Transparency mode ( or Ambient sound mode as Sony calls it) works well, but to be honest, if transparency mode is really important to you, Apple AirPods Pro are utterly amazing —the best).  I have always been a Bose guy and it was hard to imagine that these could challenge my Bose Bias from the past.

title: Difficult to connect:useless.Buy something else.
text:
Feb 2021 Update: These headphones are difficult to pair with my laptop.
Good Noise cancelling. Music sound is a bit heavy on the bass. When on Zoom the voice of other people does sound a bit muffled. Fairly comfortable to wear, being over the ear they do feel a bit warm in hot environments. Battery life is amazing. Auto sound asjustment software is really annoying with multiple noise cancelling adjustments and changes and also beeping sound warnings so I disabled it.
June 2021 Update: Headphones are difficult to pair my laptop and tablet. The sony app is updated my devices are all new. Software is VERY ANNOYING- I would return this headphones if I could, will probably end up in the trash.

title: These are worse than XM3s were at NC
text: I've been pretty loyal to the XM lineup for about 5 years now. Every once in a while I'm dumb enough to lose them somewhere or they get stolen, so I might upgrade to the next or skip a generation. The last one I had was the XM3. I was excited, since I thought it was a no brainer that the Xm4 would be even better. Logically that tracks, right? After having these for about 6 months though, I really wish I could get my money back. I can't speak to specifics of high or low frequencies and all that audiophile nerd stuff, but overall without a doubt these are substantially worse at noise cancelling than the last was, and it's very frustrating. The XM3s were a DREAM--I didn't hear a damn thing. With these XM4s, I can make out entire sentences of conversation when the volume is at a medium level. In fact, the only time I can shut out noise is if I bump the volume level almost all the way up, and I can shut the outside world out with max volume with any pair of headphones, ANC or not, so what's

title: Better than "Beats".
text: More comfortable, better built and better sound quality than "Beats".

title: Great Headphones
text: I gave these to my grandson (23) for Xmas.  He wears them almost constantly - when he's not working of course.  He says they have much better sound quality than the Bose he had before.

title: A HUGE step up from the WHXB900N
text:
I previously owned the WHXB900N headphones from Sony, and my experience with them was good. I decided to take the chance to upgrade to the WH-1000XM4, and I am glad that I did. The WH-1000XM4 is a major upgrade, and a lot of my complaints about the WHXB900N have been addressed in the WH-1000XM4.

First of all, the packaging for the WH-1000XM4 is great. You get a carrying case with accessories and the headphones. There is space created in the case to store the headphones flat as well as the extra cables and connectors, as well as a handy guide that shows the touch functions on the headphones.

My headphones had about a 1/2 charge on them. I already had the Headphones Connect app downloaded as well as Music Center, which are the two apps that work with the headphones. You will register the headphones and adjust the settings with Headphones Connect, and then you can extend the features further through the Music Center app. I had no issues connecting the app to the head

title: The best headphones for a reason
text: Bought once I was stuck working from home and have no regrets. Pretty much the perfect headset with the noise cancelling. The ability to just start talking and have the cancelling come off is also super valuable when working from home with others living there. Comfy enough too to stay on for an entire days work and the travel case is great for packing it up at the end and obviously travel

title: Sound quality is amazing!
text: My husband spent weeks researching noise canceling headphones and decided to purchase the Sony WH-1000M4. After using his headphones, I had to have a set of my own! I’ve taken them on several flights and even with crying babies I don’t hear a thing except for my music. Love them so much!!!

title: Headband Too big
text:
I’ve never had a problem with Sony headphones before until this pair. Apparently they stop making them for people with child-size heads like myself. I had to buy this god-awful ugly adapter just so they will fit properly because they’re just way too big and slide off my head. What the heck Sony!
Sound quality is amazing... if you're using it with one of the very few apps that with with 3D sound. I didn’t realize that amazing sound quality wasn’t available for just listening to music through normal apps. I really struggled to give them 3 stars but that 3D sound is pretty amazing. I just won’t be wearing these out in public unless I will have a cap on they don’t slide off.

title: Really Nice Headphones
text: These were top-rated, so I had high hopes.  The instant I placed them on my ears I knew these exceeded my expectations - They oozed luxury and comfort.  My Dad had a pair of Bose, he liked those but he loves these, which he has appropriated.

title: Best headphones for the price.
text: This was my first pair of higher end headphones and I am beyond impressed. These things are amazing. Noise canceling is really nice, sound quality is great, bass is there but nothing crazy, and the controls/app allow for customization and easy control. Battery life is top teir as well. Dare I say these are the best consumer level headphones on the market.

title: No treble, bad midrange, good bass
text: I used several HD players and all did not have the treble I expected and have with the Bose. Sound is muffled.

title: 4 Stars For Now
text:
I have been reading about the mx3 and mx4 for what seems like forever. So I have been anticipating amazing sound as well as noise cancellation. Well the noise cancellation is definitely amazing. I'm still trying to find the best sound configuration and when that happens these will be perfect but until then 4 stars.
I think these headphones are comfortable and pretty light (more so than my Beats). They charge quickly. They are nice looking. The hard sided case is functional and slimmer than expected. I look forward to tossing these in my personal item on a nice long flight somewhere.
Buying these during the Prime sale was perfect.

title: Love it!
text: I had purchased 1000XM3 just few days before the launch date of 1000XM4. Thanks to Amazon, I immediately decided to return the 1000XM3 and order the much improved and latest XM4. For me, one of the main selling point is the multi device connect at the same time. This was missing in XM3 and was a hassle when working from home and switching between work and personal stuff and multiple devices. The sound quality, noise cancellation and 360 relaity is great as well!

title: Only works for about a year!
text: For the first 6 months these headphones are great. Very easy to pair and control from the headphone cup. After 6 months a clicking sound starts from either the left or right cup. After 9 months to a year the ANC when set to the highest setting emits a high pitched squelch. Don't ever waste your time trying to get Sony to honor their warranty. They have you submit a receipt of purchase and then they just tell you to get the vendor to honor the warranty.

title: Bluetooth fails often / doesn't pair
text: Worked on my PC but then stopped working / pairing with my other devices (tested with a laptop, two smartphones and a tablet). I have much cheaper Bluetooth devices that actually work.

title: Great for music, poor for video conferences/calls
text:
The noise reduction on this is spooky good. It takes a little time to learn all the features (I recommend a youtube video or two, as the manual is tedious.) The cup to hear ambient sounds feature is genius, and being able to connect to multiple devices simultaneously is wonderful.

The one disappointment is the mics as used for video conferencing. I had hoped to use this to replace my Plantronics headset with boom mic. It works okay for this but not nearly as good as a boom headset. I thought a 5-mic array would be killer for Zoom/Team calls. Others say I sound digital/tinny on it. But I'm not returning it for this.

Comfort is only 7/10. I find them to be a slight bit tight on my big head, but I'm comparing them to my Kokoon headphones which are the most comfortable headphones in the world. They're a bit tight and the earpads a bit stiff I guess. My standards are high here.

title: The day the music died.
text:
I put the head phones on, start my favorite song, the sound is great and I can't even hear the electric heater that has been running nonstop all winter.

The song gets to the good part and I start to sing "Bye bye Miss American Pie!" and then BEEPand the music stops and I can hear the heater all over again. I look at my phone, the music is still going but I can't hear it. I restart the music, nothing. I power cycle the head phones, music starts up again, the heater can't be heard, I start to sing "Bye Bye" and then beep, no music.

After some digging I find a setting in the app, an absurd thing called "Speak to chat". Who needs to speak to people while their noise canceling headphones are on? Take the phones off you sociopath!

So I turn off the feature and everything is great for a few days until it turns on again. It's like having kids around and having them gank the radio whenever you try to sing. "Daddy Daddy you sound like a frog!" it's a cute thing f

title: My favorite headphones of all time
text:
I've tried quite a few of headphones, especially recently. These headphones are by far the best of all. I got into the WH series headphones for the first time with the XM2's. Once I got those I decided I needed headphones of that quality for the rest of my life. I upgraded to the XM3's when they came out, and now the XM4's.

The noise cancellation on these things is unbeatable. It always has been with the Sony WH headphones. My favorite feature on the new version is the automatic pause when someone starts talking. Sometimes my girlfriend yells for me or comes in my office while I'm working and starts talking and these make it so much easier to hear her the first time.

They are so lightweight and the charge lasts an incredible amount of time. I listen for hours every day and only have to charge them occasionally. If they die, you can plug them in for 10-15 minutes and get a couple more hours of music in before finishing the day and charging them overnight.

title: I can't hear my wife anymore! Thank you!!!!!!!!
text: I really hope she doesn't see this! Great headphones, but if you are looking at these I am sure you already know that...

title: Overpriced Gimmick Machine
text: The noise cancelling is great. However, I don't find the sound quality to be good. For the price they sound pretty bad. All the money went into the noise cancelling which is not a feature I think is worth even close to this price.

title: Useless with Apple computers
text: Look it up if you don't believe me. The latest Sony headphones don't work with mac computers. You'll run into very recurring sync issues (audio lags). It's a known problem and there's no firmware solution since it's a hardware issue.

title: love these
text: i bought these for my wife but i have pretty much taken them over. here is why they are awesome. normally when i exercise (for example on a spin bike) i watch TV with the sound turned way up because there is so much noise in the room (fans air cleaner A.C. the bike or treadmill etc.) now i put amazon prime or netflix on my iphone, hook the iphone up to the TV, bluetooth the sound to my headphones for the best sound i have ever heard. better than any movie theater.  the noise reduction completely gets rid of the room noise.  and...incredibly...when i pull the headphone off the movie pauses!  just got off a facetime with my mom - not only could i hear her really well she could hear me better than anything i have tried in the past (sennheiser headset, iphone mike, apple earbuds). totally worth it.

title: Fine electronics, awful physical apparatus.
text: Electronically Sony has this down. Excellent noise cancellation. The problem is with the very poorly engineered physical apparatus that totally offsets the benefits. Squeaky headband and ear pad issues make the entire experience annoying and frustrating. If you hold your head completely still like a statue you may avoid the horrible extraneous noise. Also the ear pads have flattened noticeably after a few weeks.

title: Excellent Sounds, terrible connectivity
text:
I cant knock their performance, the sound is frankly excellent.

The noise cancellation can be a little annoying because it jumps around a lot without reason or rhyme.

However, the bluetooth connectivity is terrible, it's awful. There is a delay between the headphones saying "connected" which leads to me embarrassing myself in public playing music through my speaker. My $20 bluetooth earbuds that I workout with are far more reliable and connect to my phone much smoother. A generic earbud should not beat these in any category, it's ridiculous. Don't even try to connect these to multiple devices, you'll just get a headache. The app is frankly stupid and a hassle.

Two years in, they're very annoying. I don't think I would ever buy another pair of Sony headphones, maybe not another Sony product. Good sound, but terrible design and many of its functions are below standard. $300 headphones should not be gladly replaced by $50 ones as I am doing today.

title: Best ANC in the business.
text:
Amazing sound quality and top teir ANC.

Live in a neighborhood with a lot of shootings get these and you'll never be a witness as you didn't hear anything.

title: Not perfect but one of the best sounding ANC headphones
text: The noise cancelling is probably the best I have experienced but the audio quality and comfort is average. My vmodas has better comfort, clarity and bass. The build quality is also subpar as it’s plastic. The reason I picked this up was for the ANC and it doesn’t disappoint on that end. If you beed ANC with decent sound quality, this is for you. Lots of ANC Bluetooth headphone sound quality is garbage so in comparison, the XM4 makes for a better purchase overall. Custom EQ is always a plus as well. I hope they last me a long time as it isn’t exactly the cheapest thing out there.

title: I don't know how people give these even 2 stars
text: I have no idea how these headphones have more than a 2 star rating. They disconnect from my phone every single time I get a text message, or adjust my mask, or touch the headphones at all, or move my head too fast. No issues with my Bose, Jaybirds, or Anker headphones, but these just refuse to stay connected. Constantly have to toggle Bluetooth and reconnect... but they were so expensive I'll suffer through the bulls*** for a couple years, but I'll never buy another Sony product for the rest of my life.

title: Does NOT work well on 2020 iMac (and apparently many mac computers)
text: Apparently the headphones can't always connect well to mac computers. Audio and mic quality were extremely muffled and poor quality. See link below. Sony representative recommends plugging in, but this doesn't fix mic quality and also these headphones are $300 for convenience of wireless? Works on iphone but getting the correct codec on mac can require coding downloads. My mac desktop is my main computer and I already sunk an hour into trying to fix this to no avail, so I am returning and getting the Bose which seem to have no issues on mac computers.

title: Excellent, amazing noise canceling headphones
text: First pair of N/C headphones so a little bit of a learning curve.  Took a second try to pair smart phone and laptop via bluetooth, but once that is done works seamlessly and you are not tethered to the device by a wire.  Swipe volume control is nice.  Download Sony headphone app and access equalizer modes, and N/C function.  I work in a shop with loud machinery and these reduce noise to a whisper.  Sound quality is excellent, and pause mode is nice when you take the headphones off your head.  Well made and very comfortable.

title: Lasted 6 months
text: Unfortunately, These headphones  are defective and they will eventually break in the same place either on the right or left just above the ear pad. The joint that is pictured in quite a few of the reviews here. Fair warning. I take really good care of my electronics, headphones etc. The design flaw is that the plastic is holding the weight of the ear pads, and the plastic is too thin and narrow to be able to withstand regular use. Overall the sound quality, noise canceling, call quality, and clarity is unmatched, but the cheap plastic will send me to the next best, Bose. I would highly recommend steering away from these, as Sony will not replace them when they eventually break. Unless you have 278 to buy headphones every 6 months I would stay far away from these. The negative out ways the positive on this one!

title: Avoid this at all costs
text: This was a Christmas gift and at first I was amazed, being a fan of (or used to be a fan) Sony products. They certainly haven't lived up to their standards. Unit does not sync with my iPhone se 2020 or my MacBook air 2020. It syncs with only my Dell laptop. You cannot even use this for music, it has a low energy crap setting. Companies are copying apple products simplicity to a point its unusable cause its hard to find which button does which.

title: Love these!!
text: Best quality sound! Stays charged for 24 hours. And unlike Airpods they can’t fall out of your ears and get lost!!

title: Best money I have ever spent. Over a year later.
text: So comfortable. Sound quality far better than the Bose -or any others. I purchased several sets of headphones and these were the most expensive.  But OMG, the performance and comfort of these were so far superior to the four others I tried. Was expecting more from Bose and for the $50 extra the sound quality and comfort of Sony headphones absolutely blew me away. I use these every single night and often fall asleep with them on. Best $350 I've ever spent. UPDATE ONE YEAR LATER- These headphones along with Insight Timer and Calm are the best money I've ever spent. When I put these headphones on I can tune out everything. I mean everything. The relaxation response is amazing. I have gone from a chronic insomniac to falling asleep with these head phones on and freaking out if I can't immediately find them.

title: Good headphone but not suitable for classical music
text: Great noise canceling, but fake sound for classical music, it seems that the sound loses its original definition and sounds like electric music, returned. If you use it for anything else then that's a great one, though.

title: Sounds pretty good!
text:
What I like about these headphones are the build quality in my opinion.

Pros
- the sound and bass quality are crystal clear
-it’s very comfortable on my head (light)
-the app allows me to controls the headphone settings like turning into a low res song into a high quality song with better bass.
-connecting two Bluetooth devices is very useful I never had any problems with mines
-music pause when I take off my headphones like the information from the box.
-the music lower down while speak mode is on
-headphones can update system firmware in the app
-the noise canceling is pretty good because it mutes every loud sound I hear around my background.

Cons-
Sometimes tv noise mute my headphones but I can turn it off in settings.

The people in the review are right! It is worth the money getting these headphones because i love it anyway! No problems at all.
This is my first expensive Sony Headphones and I definit

title: BEST NOISE CANCELING HEADPHONES EVER !
text:
Excellent sound, easy to set up, easy to use. Very comfortable. Still leaning some of the advanced features.
Expensive, but as always you get what you pay for. Now I have The VERY BEST.

title: My son loves these!
text: Great for computer online noise cancelling. My son bought them when they were on sale and with Prime, they arrived the same day! It was awesome! He put them on my ears so that I could hear how well the noise cancelling works, and they are GREAT!

title: Zen
text: These are absolutely worth the money. I needed to find something that would drown out my neighbor’s base and these did the trick! I was happily surprised at how quiet it can get when wearing these with or without any sound coming through. Noise canceling is perfect in my book. Good sound quality and connects well with devices. Somewhat warm after long periods of use.

title: The best you can buy for non-sport contexts
text: It's a little warm once you've worn them by a while, specially if you live in a non-cold country/place (however, not bad for a close-back headphones I guess). It fits well to the head with the right pressure and sounds great, although you shouldn't headbang with these on. The improvable stuff could be the microphone sound and a more intuitive sound control which is not bad once you get use to honestly. Finally you just can't go wrong with these.

title: Exceeded My Very High Expectations
text:
I have reviewed a number of pairs of headphones for Amazon over the years. These take the top spot in terms of audio quality and noise cancellation.

-=Build Quality=-

These are extremely comfortable, lightweight cans which still have the premium feel I would expect from headphones at this price point. They have Sony's customary, conservative styling, and collapse nicely into the (included) high-quality case.

-=Setup and Bluetooth=-

Pairing is very fast, and the headphones had no issues at all staying connected to my Android phone.

-=Software=-

Configuring the app takes a little longer than the norm because, if you want to activate the proprietary 360 technology, the app asks to take pictures of your ears and send them to Sony (seriously). Whether or not this accomplishes anything is a mystery to me, and the step is completely optional. The headphones sound great either way.

The equalizer works very well, allow

title: Worth it
text: There very good headphones worth the money sound is out of this world

title: The Penultimate Consumer Bluetooth Headphones!!
text: The penultimate consumer Bluetooth headphones!! For the last 5 years Sony's XM headphones have been the best consumer headphones on the market. Check it out if you don't believe me. People go nuts about Apple's, but the honest reviewers tell you they aren't worth the money. These run against the Bose, and reviewers throw Bose a bone or two, but these always walk away with the prize. Easy to use, the best sound cancelation and very light and comfortable. I can work in my office and it drowns out the TV and noise in the next room, even without music playing. I went with the blue and glad I did. It adds a little color while not being ostentatious. :)

title: Great sound, excellent noise reduction.
text:
I bought the Sony XM4 headphones to replace an older set of Sony studio monitors based primarily on the reviews on-line. I wanted great sound, and noise cancellation. These headphones do not disappoint.

I use my headphones to listen to lps (at the moment) with a good audio source (Technics 1210GR/Blue Point2). I've listened to many different music types, and I like the fact that the sound is balanced and warm. If I listened to more rock I might want a stronger bass presentation, but I tend towards jazz and classical and I prefer accuracy and balance.

I'm using a low end Sony stereo receiver and the sound over paired Bluetooth connections is not as dynamic and strong as a direct plug connection. In my office I run a fan, and this set takes the fan out of the background. Just great. Reviews suggest the Bose high end is equally good for noise cancellation.

This is an enclosed over the ears headset. The pads fit snugly, but not tight. I wear my MX4 for hour

title: One bad item
text: As a former club dj and audiophile I have used dozens of headsets over the years. I have to admit that for the price point these deliver excellent sound, the dual connectivity is a major plus as they connected to my win10 PC and Samsung ultra 21 with zero problems. Noise cancellation works very well and the bass is perfect for me. They are also comfortable without feeling like they are pinching my head, I have had zero heating issues. The ear cups are comfortable and fit snugly. My only issue and someone mentioned this as well, the ruddy touch sensor sucks, PERIOD. Samsung just needs to go back to physical buttons and make us a lot happier. Other than that I highly recommend these for anyone that really enjoys music. Oh yeah the call quality is excellent and I have ranged mine out to 25 feet without losing signal.

title: Great protection from full blast airline announcements
text: These work great on airplanes and you can’t even hear the blaring announcements. And you just put your palm against the right headphone to be able to hear ambient noise. You can control the volume and skip tracks just by swiping the right headphone. Easy blue tooth connection.

title: FAILED IN JUST 8 MONTHS - NORMAL USE
text: THE THING JUST SNAPPED. normal use, nothing crazy, it just broke, and SONY refuses to cover it.

title: Excellent headphones for studying, jamming, and exercise
text: Excellent sound quality with perfect bass and midrange, with good treble. Noise cancelation is exceptional for studying and listening to music while working out. Comfortable to wear ALL DAY LONG...I use it everyday. Even though it's not rated sweat proof, I've had no issue. Worth the price.

title: Have some discomfort in the fitting of the ear pads
text: That's why I gave it the 4.  Also. Music does not stop for voice recognition like bose will. Still works for what I need.

title: Worth the Hype
text:
loads of videos and reviews by the techies on YouTube and everywhere else. Dual device connection is a fantastic feature and the implementation is excellent. i have many devices connected and the process of dropping one and connecting the one i need is really easy

I wont go into details of build quality and design, you can easily find those on the youtube reviews. what i can say is if this is within your budget, you wont regret your purchase

title: Excellent Headphones
text: Multi-purpose headphones since you can use them with your PC, cell phone or nothing at all just for the noise canceling feature. Light weight and very comfortable.

title: Not for me
text:
First, I don't like that 99% of the instruction is dedicated to Apple devices. 1% of instruction is dedicated to instruction for Android .
The product assumes adept and experienced consumer and presents somewhat smugly for the young users. Speaking from the perspective of a technologically challenged person, I would have been lost setting this up without input from teenagers.
